title: rubus × minoriflorus
intro: Rubus × minoriflorus, also known as the black raspberry or thimble raspberry, is a hybrid plant species in the genus Rubus, resulting from a cross between Rubus idaeus and Rubus occidentalis. The plant's leaves and fruits possess medicinal properties, primarily due to their high content of anthocyanins and ellagic acid, which have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Rubus × minoriflorus include its potential to reduce the risk of certain cancers, improve cardiovascular health, and protect against neurodegenerative diseases. Rubus × minoriflorus is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and capsules, which can be consumed to reap its potential health benefits.
